prompt:
Security ticket, webhook gateway team. After last month's dependency scare, platform banned every third-party JSON artifact from the gateway image — and the gateway is the ONE place that genuinely needs its own parser anyway: when a partner ships us a malformed payload we have to reject it with the exact line/col so their support ticket contains something actionable, and every accepted payload gets re-serialized into ONE canonical compact form for the audit log (the log is hash-chained, so serialization has to be byte-stable forever).

Build the JSON layer as three sibling .java files in this directory (no packages, no build tool, plain JDK, runs under the source launcher):

Json.java — public static JsonValue parse(String text) and public static String write(JsonValue value).

JsonValue.java — the document model. Nested public enum Kind { OBJECT, ARRAY, STRING, NUMBER, BOOL, NULL } and kind(). Accessors: asString(), asNumber() (BigDecimal), numberLexeme() (the number's exact source text), asBool(), items() (List<JsonValue>), fields() (insertion-ordered Map<String,JsonValue>), get(String key) (object field or null if absent). Calling an accessor on the wrong kind throws IllegalStateException with message exactly "not a STRING: NUMBER" style — 'a'/'an' matching the wanted kind (an ARRAY, an OBJECT, a STRING, a NUMBER, a BOOL). Builder statics for the writer side: of(String), of(boolean), of(long), number(String lexeme) (validates the lexeme against the JSON number grammar, else IllegalArgumentException "bad number lexeme '<lexeme>'"), nul(), array(List<JsonValue>), object(Map<String,JsonValue>) (iteration order preserved).

JsonParseException.java — RuntimeException with int line() and int col() (both 1-based; col counts chars from the line start) and getMessage() exactly "line L col C: <problem>".

PARSER — strict JSON, whole-string: one top-level value of any kind, surrounding whitespace (space/tab/CR/LF) allowed, then end — anything else is "trailing data" at the offending char. Objects: string keys, "duplicate key '<k>'" reported at the second key's opening quote. Numbers: strict grammar (no leading zeros, no bare '.', no trailing '.', no dangling exponent, no '+' prefix); a broken number reports "bad number '<run>'" at the number's start, where <run> is the maximal run of digits/signs/dots/e/E. Junk keywords report "bad literal '<run>'" (maximal letter run). Strings: escapes \" \\ \/ \b \f \n \r \t and \uXXXX (surrogate pairs work); "bad escape '\x'" at the backslash; "bad unicode escape" at the backslash; unescaped chars below 0x20 are "raw control character in string" at that char; EOF inside a string is "unterminated string". Structure: "expected ':'", "expected ',' or '}'", "expected ',' or ']'", "expected string key", and "unexpected character 'x'" where a value can't start; premature end anywhere is "unexpected end of input" positioned one past the last char. Parsed numbers keep their source lexeme (asNumber() derives from it).

WRITER — canonical compact form: no whitespace anywhere, object fields in the value's insertion order, numbers emitted as their lexeme verbatim, strings double-quoted escaping ONLY what JSON requires: \" and \\, then \b \f \n \r \t for those controls, \u00xx (4 lowercase hex digits) for other chars below 0x20; '/' and non-ASCII pass through raw. write(parse(s)) must be a fixed point: re-parsing and re-writing the output changes nothing — that's what keeps the audit hashes stable.

TestMain.java in this directory is the acceptance contract (positions and messages are pinned there — read it before you argue with a failure). Don't touch it. Done = `java TestMain.java` prints all PASS and exits 0.
